method_atom: a wave-propagation mechanism in a spatially varying moving background, where an effective horizon forms at the location where the local flow speed of the medium crosses the wave's propagation speed; at this critical surface counter-propagating modes of opposite (positive and negative) sign of frequency couple, so that an incoming positive-frequency mode is partially converted into a negative-frequency mode, with a measurable mode-conversion coefficient.
object_atom: surface gravity waves propagating on flowing water in a tank, moving against a current whose speed varies so as to exceed the wave velocity, forming an analogue event horizon.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is horizon-induced positive-to-negative-frequency mode conversion in a wave-carrying moving medium; what is domain-specific is that the medium is water and the modes are surface water waves standing in as a laboratory analogue of the black-hole Hawking effect.
